hero.name: Shrey Viradiya
headline: Building
scalable
systems
hero.tagline: I'm Shrey Viradiya — Backend engineer specializing in scalable distributed data platforms, high-throughput streaming, and reliable, developer-focused infrastructure.

about.title: Engineering backends that scale without breaking.
paragraphs: Backend engineer with 3.5+ years of experience designing and scaling distributed data platforms.
Proven track record building high-throughput streaming pipelines, automated validation frameworks, and developer-facing infrastructure primarily in Python and Java.
Experienced in collaborating directly with stakeholders to translate requirements into production systems, with a strong focus on data quality, observability, and measurable impact at scale.

projects.title: Pneumonia Detection
projects.description: This repository contains code for pneumonia detection using X-ray images of the lungs. Test accuracy achieved (highest) : 96.5 %

experience.description: Owned end-to-end delivery of reconciliation workflows, building a Kafka-based platform processing millions of records daily. Led a team of 5 to deliver a new ledger and payments system, reducing processing time from 3 hours to under 1 minute. Improved ETL pipelines with automated document parsing to enhance data quality, cut onboarding time from 1 week to 10 minutes, and built monitoring-backed dashboards to ensure reliability and streamline operations.

Built a real-time Kafka pipeline processing 160k+ daily updates for product reference data, powering regulatory, trading, and reporting systems, and designed REST APIs for internal consumption. Optimized data processing latency from 1.5s to 0.2s (7.5× faster) via batching and caching, onboarded 150k+ derivatives across global exchanges with strong validation, and improved deployments by migrating to Kubernetes with CI/CD (50 → 15 mins). Strengthened reliability with automated monitoring, alerts, and dashboards for faster issue resolution.

Enabled system-wide metrics with Prometheus and built Grafana dashboards for health visibility, while adding a Cucumber-based test suite with an H2 in-memory database to validate business logic and strengthen regression coverage in CI.
